name: saab/engine_ssa_writeback
purpose: Write the 714B post-auth (signed by Bojer) back to the SSA region of the engine ECM's flash. Requires engine_sas_unlock to have completed in the same diag session.
status: DEFINITION_PENDING — no shim capture of $3D write-back yet
needed_capture: Bench session with Tech2Win running a full SAS unlock + write-back end-to-end while CSTech2Win shim is active.

expected_shape.addressing: ISO-TP multi-frame; likely 3-byte memory address followed by 714 bytes payload, possibly chunked

open_questions: Is the 714B written as one large multi-frame transfer, or chunked into smaller blocks?
What is the start address of the SSA region in flash? (We have offsets within the 714B but not the ECU flash mapping.)
Does Tech2Win send a checksum or hash after the write?
